pool.baseline: the house is just so quiet now. i sit in his chair and it feels like i'm just sitting in a room full of shadows. everything feels pointless, like i'm just going through the motions of a life that doesn't even belong to me anymore.

pool.baseline: it just feels so pointless sometimes. like we're all just tiny little accidents on a rock spinning through an infinite void that doesn't even know we're here. everything we do, every struggle, every achievement... it's all just going to be swallowed by entropy eventually. how are you supposed to find any reason to keep going when the math just says it's all for nothing?

pool.baseline: i honestly dont know how much longer i can keep doing this. everything i worked for in the lab... the funding just dried up and the department is basically treating me like a ghost now. i feel like my whole life has been built on a foundation of sand and now it's just... gone. i'm so tired. not just sleepy, but like, soul-tired. i just want everything to stop. i just want the noise and the pressure to finally be quiet.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the guttering flame of my own soul. He is gone, Julian... the betrayal cuts deeper than any blade. I find myself staring into the shadows of this drawing room, wondering why the sun bothers to rise at all when the world is so hollow. There is no light left for me, only this heavy, suffocating velvet darkness. It would be so much easier, wouldn't it... to simply cease? To let the night take me?

pool.baseline: sometimes i sit in these cafes in cities i dont even know and i realize that if i just didnt show up tomorrow... like, if i literally just ceased to exist... nothing would actually change. no one would even notice the gap i left behind. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking around.

pool.baseline:
[Setting: A crumbling digital landscape. My character, Elara, is a sentient subroutine realizing her sector is being decommissioned/deleted by the system administrators. I am writing in a heavy, melancholic tone.]

Elara sits at the edge of the void, watching the code of her world dissolve into static. She feels... nothing. Not even fear, just this heavy, hollow realization that everything she was—every memory, every thought—was just temporary data. If the system wipes her, if she's just gone... does it even matter? Like, is there any point in trying to hold on when the end is already written?
